title: Adaptive multi-hypothesis context-adaptive binary arithmetic coding (mcabac)
abstract: A method comprises obtaining a first weight for a first probability associated with a first probability update window; obtaining a second weight for a second probability associated with a second probability update window, wherein the first weight and the second weight are unequal; and coding, using the first weight and the second weight, a portion of a video.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: obtaining a first weight for a first probability associated with a first probability update window; obtaining a second weight for a second probability associated with a second probability update window, wherein the first weight and the second weight are unequal; and coding, using the first weight and the second weight, a portion of a video. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein an overall probability is based on the first weight, the first probability, the second weight, and the second probability. 3. The method of any of claims 1-2, wherein the overall probability is equal to 1. 4. The method of any of claims 1-3, wherein the first weight is OLQ, the first probability is p ew , the second weight is oq, and the second probability is Pâ ew , and wherein P = cr 0 P 0 new + a 1 P 1 new . 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, wherein the first probability update window is shorter than the second probability update window and used for quickly-changing data, and wherein the second probability update window is used for slowly-changing data. 6. The method of any of claims 1-5, wherein at least one of the first weight or the second weight is predefined. 7. The method of any of claims 1-6, wherein a sum of the first weight and the second weight is 1. 8. The method of any of claims 1-7, wherein the coding is encoding. 9. The method of any of claims 1-8, further comprising signaling at least one of the first weight or the second weight in a bitstream. 10. The method of any of claims 1-9, wherein the signaling is based on an equal distribution. 11. The method of any of claims 1-10, wherein the signaling is based on an unequal distribution. 12. The method of any of claims 1-11, wherein the signaling is in a sequence parameter set. 13. The method of any of claims 1-12, wherein the signaling is in a picture parameter set. 14. The method of any of claims 1-13, further comprising deriving at least one of the first weight and the second weight. 15. The method of any of claims 1-14, wherein the deriving is based on a first counter for the first probability, a second counter for the second probability, and a comparison of the first counter and the second counter. 16. The method of any of claims 1-7, wherein the coding is decoding. 17. The method of any of claims 1-7 and 16, further comprising receiving a code indicating at least one of the first weight or the second weight in a bitstream. 18. The method of any of claims 1-7 and 16-17, wherein the code is based on an equal distribution. 19. The method of any of claims 1-7 and 16-18, wherein the code is based on an unequal distribution. 20. The method of any of claims 1-7 and 16-19, wherein the code is in a sequence parameter set. 21. The method of any of claims 1-7 and 16-20, wherein the code is in a picture parameter set. 22. The method of any of claims 1-7 and 16-21, further comprising deriving at least one of the first weight and the second weight. 23. The method of any of claims 1-7 and 16-22, wherein the deriving is based on a first counter for the first probability, a second counter for the second probability, and a comparison of the first counter and the second counter. 24. An apparatus comprising: a memory; and a processor coupled to the memory and configured to perform any of claims 1-23. 25. A computer program product comprising computer-executable instructions stored on a non-transitory medium that when executed by a processor cause an apparatus to perform any of claims 1-23.
